Title: Housing costs of households; household and dwelling characteristics
Summary:
Housing costs, housing cost ratio, skewed rent
Owner or tenant, household characteristics, dwelling characteristics
ShortDescription:
This table contains figures on the housing costs of private households in independent homes. Households living (temporarily) in a house free of charge are not included. The figures are presented for both owners and tenants and can be further divided into various characteristics of the household and the dwelling.

Data available from: 2012

Status of the figures: final

Changes as of Januari 12, 2026
The table is stopped.

When will new figures be published?
Not applicable.
Description: CONTENTS 1. General information 2. Definitions and explanation of symbols 3. Links to relevant tables and articles 4. Sources and methods 5. Further information 1. GENERAL INFORMATION This table contains figures on the housing costs of private households in independent homes. Households living (temporarily) in a house free of charge are not included. The figures are presented for both owners and tenants and can be further divided into various characteristics of the household and the dwelling. Data available from: 2012 Status of the figures: final Changes as of Januari 12, 2026 The table is stopped. When will new figures be published? Not applicable. 2. DEFINITIONS AND EXPLANATION OF SYMBOLS Private household: One or more persons living in a dwelling together and meeting their own daily needs (i.e. not as companies). Independent home: A complete dwelling, i.e. a dwelling occupied by only one household with facilities including toilet, bathroom and kitchen which are not shared with other households. Explanation of symbols: Empty cell : figure not applicable . : figure is unknown, insufficiently reliable or confidential * : provisional figure ** : revised provisional figure Figures are not published (.) in WoON when point estimate is based on fewer than 30 observations. 3. LINKS TO RELEVANT TABLES AND ARTICLES Relevant tables: Housing costs of households; household or dwelling characteristics and region Relevant articles: More information can be found at the theme page Construction and housing. 4. SOURCES AND METHODS The Netherlands’ Housing Survey (WoON) is a three-yearly survey on the housing situation of the Dutch population and their living requirements and needs. Elements covered include the composition of households, the dwelling and living environment, housing costs, living requirements and housing relocations. The WoON survey is a sample survey. This implies that the figures published are estimates. The accuracy of these estimates is indicated in the table by the ‘95% confidence interval’. This interval contains the true (unknown) value with 95% certainty. The survey method for this table can be found in the survey description of Netherlands’ Housing Survey (WoON). 5.
